content.full_text:
May 14, 2007

Rick Ingraham, Manager
Children and Family Services Branch
Dept. Developmental Services
1600 Ninth Street, Rm. 330, MS3-8
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-077

Dear Mr. Ingraham:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 Please note that we base our advice solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090. We recommend that you seek appropriate counsel with respect to these provisions.

As we discussed in our telephone conversation on May 9, 2007, you do not have a particular decision currently facing you on which we can offer advice. Accordingly, no response to your letter is necessary at this time and you have withdrawn your request for advice. The Commission does not offer advice if a request does not pose a question under the Act, or if the request is hypothetical. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), (c)(4)(D).) Should the occasion arise where you have a decision before you that could pose a potential conflict of interest, please do not hesitate to contact us for advice.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
